findings.what: console.log in production job code
findings.why: Use logger, not console. Job output should go through structured logging.
findings.fix_direction: Replace console.log with logger from the job's build input

findings.what: console.log in production event handler
findings.why: Use logger, not console.
findings.fix_direction: Replace with logger from build input

findings.what: console.error in production hook
findings.why: Use logger or silent handling. Console.error leaks to browser devtools.
findings.fix_direction: Remove or replace with proper error handler/reporter

findings.what: catchUntagged(e) without throw prefix
findings.why: Every other call site uses throw catchUntagged(e). Functionally safe (returns never), but inconsistent — looks like silent swallowing to readers.
findings.fix_direction: Add throw prefix: throw catchUntagged(e)

findings.what: catchUntagged(e) without throw prefix in traced handler wrapper
findings.why: Same as F004 — inconsistency with the rest of the codebase.
findings.fix_direction: Add throw prefix: throw catchUntagged(e)

findings.what: Guest session cookie set client-side with no HMAC/signature — tamperable
findings.why: Cross-user attribution manipulation possible
findings.fix_direction: Create session server-side, sign with GUEST_SESSION_SALT, set HttpOnly+SameSite

findings.what: portal.id reference in useEffect out of scope — guest scans broken
findings.why: Variable destructured AFTER useEffect, should be data.portal.id
findings.fix_direction: Change portal.id to data.portal.id inside useEffect

findings.what: clock.ts documented but does not exist
findings.why: Spec lies about test determinism strategy
findings.fix_direction: Create clock.ts or remove from CONTEXT.md

findings.what: 5+ files import directly from neverthrow instead of shared barrel
findings.why: Undermines shared barrel indirection
findings.fix_direction: Replace from 'neverthrow' with from '#/shared/domain'

findings.what: HTTP status codes in shared/auth/ but imported by 13+ non-auth files
findings.why: Coupling all contexts to shared/auth for non-auth concerns
findings.fix_direction: Move to src/shared/http/status.ts

findings.what: Tenant cache keyed by raw cookie header — memory amplification + collision
findings.why: Large cookie headers amplify memory, cookie changes cause cache misses
findings.fix_direction: Key by session cookie value only

findings.what: Flat useCases spread risks silent name collision between contexts
findings.why: If two contexts export same-named use case, later silently overwrites
findings.fix_direction: Namespace or add collision assertion

findings.what: error-status.ts, pubsub-jwt.verifier.ts, auth-cli.ts not in CONTEXT.md
findings.why: Spec drift
findings.fix_direction: Add missing files to CONTEXT.md auth section

findings.what: Webhook imports from infrastructure/handlers/ not documented in CONTEXT.md
findings.why: Spec only allows server/ imports for routes
findings.fix_direction: Update CONTEXT.md webhook exception to allow infrastructure/handlers/

findings.what: Module-level mutable _lookup — test isolation risk
findings.why: Stale permission data between tests if one fails mid-way
findings.fix_direction: Add resetPermissionLookup() for test teardown

findings.what: resetPasswordEmailHtml generates structurally broken HTML
findings.why: Unclosed/mismatched div tags
findings.fix_direction: Fix emailShell to properly close div.body/div.container

findings.what: deleteProperty passed as raw server fn reference, not wrapped in useMutationAction
findings.why: Violates documented pattern in CONTEXT.md
findings.fix_direction: Wrap in useMutationAction in route file

findings.what: Soft-deleted portals served to guests — findPublicPortalBySlug missing deleted_at IS NULL
findings.why: Deleted portals accessible via public slug route
findings.fix_direction: Add deleted_at IS NULL filter to portal query

findings.what: Smart routing threshold: spec says 1-5, code enforces 1-4
findings.why: Spec/code mismatch — could be functional bug
findings.fix_direction: Align code and spec

findings.what: 6 use cases have zero test coverage (request-upload, finalize-upload, get-portal-qr, get-portal, list-portals, list-portal-groups)
findings.why: Untested code is untrusted code
findings.fix_direction: Add test files for all 6

findings.what: validateUrl allows javascript: and data: schemes
findings.why: XSS risk if use case bypassed
findings.fix_direction: Require https: scheme in domain constructor

findings.what: portal-group repo, mapper, link-resolver, s3 adapter, process-image job have zero tests
findings.why: Recently modified code with no test coverage
findings.fix_direction: Add integration tests

findings.what: Upload handlers swallow errors without catchUntagged
findings.why: S3 errors become opaque 'Upload request failed' messages
findings.fix_direction: Use throw catchUntagged(e) after portalError branch

findings.what: Missing portalGroupToRow mapper — repo does mapping inline
findings.why: Violates mapper pattern, other mappers have FromRow+ToRow pairs
findings.fix_direction: Add portalGroupToRow to mapper

findings.what: incrementProgress lacks tenant isolation — no orgId filter
findings.why: Cross-tenant progress manipulation theoretically possible
findings.fix_direction: Add orgId parameter, JOIN on goals table to verify ownership

findings.what: findAllActive queries ALL tenants with no safeguard preventing request-scoped use
findings.why: If called from server fn, returns cross-tenant data
findings.fix_direction: Rename to findAllActiveForAllTenants() or add branded type guard

findings.what: on-group-deleted handler has zero test coverage
findings.why: Destructive operation (cancels goals) untested
findings.fix_direction: Add on-group-deleted.test.ts

findings.what: No integration test for goal repository — zero tenant isolation test coverage
findings.why: 20+ repo methods untested against real DB
findings.fix_direction: Add goal.repository.test.ts with integration tests

findings.what: incrementProgress is dead code — only upsertProgress is called
findings.why: 85 lines of dead SQL + stubs in every test file
findings.fix_direction: Remove from port and repo

findings.what: cancel-goal emits no domain event
findings.why: Other contexts can't react to cancellations
findings.fix_direction: Add goal.cancelled event or document why intentionally event-free

findings.what: InboxNote create() trusts note.organizationId without explicit orgId param
findings.why: Defense-in-depth gap — other repos require explicit orgId
findings.fix_direction: Add orgId param and verify match

findings.what: Raw sql template for rating filters instead of Drizzle gte/lte operators
findings.why: Bypasses type-safe query builder
findings.fix_direction: Use gte()/lte() from drizzle-orm

findings.what: Missing unbrand() on ReviewId in on-reply-published, inconsistent with on-review-updated
findings.why: Style inconsistency
findings.fix_direction: Add unbrand(event.reviewId)

findings.what: inboxItemStatusChanged event missing propertyId field
findings.why: Downstream handlers may expect propertyId
findings.fix_direction: Add propertyId to event payload

findings.what: 6 of 7 reply mutation server functions skip can() permission gate
findings.why: Inconsistent with getReplyFn and architecture pattern authorize-validate-execute
findings.fix_direction: Add can(ctx.role, 'reply.manage') to all mutation server functions

findings.what: findAllExpiringBefore/findAllExpiredBefore are cross-tenant with no type guard
findings.why: Returns full domain objects from all orgs — fragile
findings.fix_direction: Add SECURITY comment, consider stripped return type

findings.what: deleteReply only allows draft status — rejected replies must be edited-then-deleted
findings.why: Inconsistent UX
findings.fix_direction: Allow deleting rejected replies or document as intentional

findings.what: Staff recent activity pulls 500 rows then sorts in JS to take 5
findings.why: Wasteful at scale
findings.fix_direction: Add findRecentByPropertyId with ORDER BY LIMIT at DB level

findings.what: syncReviews bypasses buildReview constructor, skips rating validation for GBP data
findings.why: Rating 0 or 6 from Google adapter stored without validation
findings.fix_direction: Add assertion for rating range before upsert

findings.what: Unsafe type assertions for platform and rating in mapper
findings.why: Bypasses compile-time safety at boundary
findings.fix_direction: Add runtime validation

findings.what: Cross-tenant findAllExpiredBefore + deleteById — fragile if deleteById signature changes
findings.why: Safety net depends on a single parameter
findings.fix_direction: Add comment documenting cross-tenant-by-design

findings.what: 5 identity server functions (inviteMember, resend, updateRole, remove, listInvitations) skip can() gate
findings.why: Defense-in-depth failure — other identity endpoints have can() checks
findings.fix_direction: Add can() checks to all 5 server functions

findings.what: changePassword, updateProfile, updateUserImage, createOrganization skip requireAuth()
findings.why: Auth bypass risk — endpoints only call getAuth() without enforcing auth
findings.fix_direction: Add await requireAuth(headers) to all 4 endpoints

findings.what: organizations.ts is 734-line god file with 15+ server functions
findings.why: Single-responsibility violation, merge conflict magnet
findings.fix_direction: Split into organizations/members/invitations/auth/uploads

findings.what: createOrganization has no permission check or rate limiting
findings.why: Any authenticated user can create unlimited organizations
findings.fix_direction: Add rate limiting or document intentional open access

findings.what: Domain events (memberInvited, memberRemoved, etc.) defined but never emitted
findings.why: Dead events — cross-context handlers can't react to member changes
findings.fix_direction: Wire events into use cases/server functions or remove from domain

findings.what: Import at line 567 — all other imports at top of file
findings.why: Maintenance hazard
findings.fix_direction: Move import to top

findings.what: Suspicious rawMembers fallback: (result?.members ?? result ?? []) as AuthMemberResponse[]
findings.why: Type assertion hides potential runtime bug
findings.fix_direction: Add explicit Array.isArray check

findings.what: auth-settings.ts uses raw error codes not in IdentityErrorCode union
findings.why: Bypasses tagged error system and exhaustive matching
findings.fix_direction: Extend IdentityErrorCode or document intentional bypass

findings.what: identity.port.better-auth.ts is a tiny re-export file — possibly dead code
findings.why: May be unused if types imported directly from better-auth
findings.fix_direction: Verify usage, remove if unused

findings.what: Duplicated trend() function across 2 files
findings.why: Drift risk
findings.fix_direction: Extract to shared domain/trend.ts

findings.what: Duplicated timeRangeToDates() and makeDashboardError across 3 server files
findings.why: Any fix requires editing 3 files
findings.fix_direction: Extract to shared server/dashboard-utils.ts

findings.what: 6 parallel SQL aggregation queries per request with no caching
findings.why: DB hammer at scale
findings.fix_direction: Add in-memory TTL cache at repo level

findings.what: getKPIsForPortals does property-level review stats, not portal-scoped — data leakage to staff
findings.why: Staff sees review KPIs from all portals, not just assigned ones
findings.fix_direction: Add portal-scoped review stats method

findings.what: reviewWhere() may not filter soft-deleted reviews
findings.why: Stale KPIs if reviews have soft-delete
findings.fix_direction: Add isNull(reviews.deletedAt) if applicable

findings.what: listStaffPortals directly accesses portalRepo — cross-context boundary violation
findings.why: Couples staff to portal repository internals
findings.fix_direction: Extract to PortalLookupPort or composition root function

findings.what: updateStaffPortals validates portals against portalRepo — same boundary violation
findings.why: Same cross-context coupling
findings.fix_direction: Move portal validation to use case via port

findings.what: Portal validation runs BEFORE try/catch — unhandled error bubbling
findings.why: If listByProperty throws, it's not caught by catchUntagged
findings.fix_direction: Move validation inside try block

findings.what: updateStaffPortals idGen returns raw string instead of branded StaffAssignmentId
findings.why: Type mismatch — should wrap with staffAssignmentId()
findings.fix_direction: Wrap with staffAssignmentId(randomUUID())

findings.what: importProperty bypasses buildProperty smart constructor — no validation
findings.why: Domain invariants (name, slug, timezone) can be violated on import
findings.fix_direction: Use buildProperty().match() in importProperty

findings.what: findByGbpPlaceId is cross-org by design — leaks org membership via gbpPlaceId
findings.why: If webhook auth bypassed, attacker can enumerate properties
findings.fix_direction: Add code-level guard or restrict caller identity

findings.what: 9 of 10 public API methods untested at build level — importProperty especially risky
findings.why: Untested wiring
findings.fix_direction: Add tests for importProperty, clearGoogleConnectionRef, findByGbpPlaceId

findings.what: PropertyCreated event has optional GBP fields never populated by createProperty
findings.why: Downstream consumers don't get gbpPlaceId in event
findings.fix_direction: Include optional fields from property in event payload

findings.what: recordScan bypasses domain constructor — raw object spread, no buildScanEvent
findings.why: Every other entity uses buildX constructor, scans skip validation
findings.fix_direction: Add buildScanEvent constructor and use it

findings.what: Guest session ID generated but never sent back as cookie — broken session for first-time visitors
findings.why: Multiple ratings possible from same guest
findings.fix_direction: Set Set-Cookie header for newly generated sessions

findings.what: Duplicate ScanSource type definition
findings.why: Two sources of truth for same type
findings.fix_direction: Import from domain/types.ts

findings.what: teamLeadId cast from string without branded ID constructor
findings.why: Bypasses branded ID system
findings.fix_direction: Use userId(input.teamLeadId) constructor

findings.what: TeamPublicApi is empty type — exported but has no methods
findings.why: Dead code that misleads consumers
findings.fix_direction: Remove or document as placeholder

findings.what: getTeam use case exists but has no server function — unreachable via HTTP
findings.why: Dead use case wiring
findings.fix_direction: Add getTeam server function to teams.ts

findings.what: propertyId as string instead of unbrand() in team repo
findings.why: Inconsistent with unbrand convention
findings.fix_direction: Replace with unbrand(propertyId)

findings.what: Metric repo doesn't use unbrand() for branded IDs in inserts
findings.why: Inconsistent — other repos all unbrand
findings.fix_direction: Add unbrand() calls for all branded IDs in insertReading

findings.what: findByOrganizationId and queryAggregate don't unbrand() IDs in WHERE
findings.why: Same as F071 — consistency
findings.fix_direction: Add unbrand() calls

findings.what: BUILT_IN_METRIC_KEYS duplicates MetricKey type — 3 places to maintain same list
findings.why: Drift risk
findings.fix_direction: Derive from single source of truth

findings.what: createMetricReading doesn't validate metricKey against known keys
findings.why: Domain should be final enforcement point
findings.fix_direction: Add metric key validation to constructor

findings.what: ActivityError missing _tag field, uses open string code — breaks tagged error convention
findings.why: No isActivityError guard, can't use exhaustive matching
findings.fix_direction: Add _tag, closed union type, isActivityError guard

findings.what: createActivityLog returns id: '' placeholder — invalid domain object
findings.why: Violates domain invariant that entities must have valid IDs
findings.fix_direction: Accept id as input parameter to constructor

findings.what: findByResource doesn't filter by organizationId — potential cross-tenant data leak
findings.why: Resource ID lookup not scoped to tenant
findings.fix_direction: Add organizationId to WHERE clause

findings.what: findDuplicate uses naive JSONB text comparison — key ordering causes false negatives
findings.why: Idempotency check can miss true duplicates
findings.fix_direction: Use Postgres @> containment operator for JSONB equality

findings.what: Reply handlers use resourceType: 'inbox_item' instead of 'reply' per CONTEXT.md
findings.why: Implementation diverges from CONTEXT.md contract
findings.fix_direction: Align handlers with CONTEXT.md or update spec

findings.what: Activity repo uses raw cast instead of unbrand — context doesn't use branded IDs at all
findings.why: No type safety against ID mixups in activity context
findings.fix_direction: Consider using branded IDs or document intentional plain strings

findings.what: tracedHandler missing method and trace name — activity functions won't appear in traces
findings.why: Missing observability
findings.fix_direction: Add method and traceName to tracedHandler calls

findings.what: Role cast from DB without validation — raw (row as Record).role as Role
findings.why: Invalid role values pass through unchecked
findings.fix_direction: Validate against VALID_ROLES set before returning

findings.what: Non-admin pagination does in-memory filtering — approximate pages, potential OOM
findings.why: Performance at scale
findings.fix_direction: Push property filtering into DB WHERE clause

findings.file: Multiple use case files across all contexts
findings.what: Inconsistent error strategy: some use cases return Result, some throw tagged errors
findings.why: No single convention — callers don't know what to expect
findings.fix_direction: Standardize on one approach (throw seems dominant)

findings.what: 28 components directly import server functions — violates CONTEXT.md no-server-imports rule
findings.why: Blurs route/component boundary, makes components untestable in isolation
findings.fix_direction: Prop-drill server fns from route files

findings.what: useServerFn() called inside components instead of route files
findings.why: Couples components to TanStack Start server fn infrastructure
findings.fix_direction: Move useServerFn calls to routes, pass bound fns as props

findings.what: organization-settings-page is a route wolf in component clothing — imports 4 server fns, uses useServerFn/useNavigate/useAction
findings.why: Sets bad precedent for other page components
findings.fix_direction: Extract server fn wiring to route file

findings.what: people-page imports 5 server functions from 3 modules — no NOTE exception comment
findings.why: Undocumented violation of no-server-imports rule
findings.fix_direction: Move data fetching to route, pass data/actions as props

findings.what: 12 components import from shared/domain/ instead of application/public-api
findings.why: Leaks domain internals into UI layer
findings.fix_direction: Re-export from public-api barrel

findings.what: Record<string, any> for property/portal objects — no type safety
findings.why: Runtime errors if shape changes
findings.fix_direction: Define proper interfaces

findings.what: File named go-create-form-state.ts — typo for goal-create-form-state.ts
findings.why: Confusing navigation
findings.fix_direction: Rename to goal-create-form-state.ts

findings.what: 15+ components export bare 'type Props' instead of component-prefixed names
findings.why: Type collisions when re-exported from barrels
findings.fix_direction: Rename to [ComponentName]Props

findings.what: reply-form imports 7 server functions directly — worst single-file offender
findings.why: Untestable without live server
findings.fix_direction: Accept mutation actions as props from route

findings.what: ReviewReplyRejected onEditResubmit is a no-op — button renders but does nothing
findings.why: Bad UX — user-facing button with no effect
findings.fix_direction: Wire up or hide button

findings.what: selectedIds.includes() is O(n) per row — O(n*m) for full list
findings.why: Performance at scale
findings.fix_direction: Convert selectedIds to Set before mapping

findings.what: Hardcoded Tailwind grays (text-gray-500, bg-white) instead of semantic theme tokens
findings.why: Breaks theming/dark mode
findings.fix_direction: Use text-muted-foreground, bg-background tokens

findings.what: connectGoogle missing can(integration.manage) — Staff role can connect Google accounts
findings.why: Permission bypass for Google account connection
findings.fix_direction: Add can() check in server fn or use case

findings.what: listGbpLocations, startPropertyImport, getImportStatus — all missing can(integration.manage)
findings.why: Staff role could trigger property imports from GBP
findings.fix_direction: Add can() checks to all 3

findings.what: listGoogleConnections missing can() — any org member can see Google connection details
findings.why: Staff shouldn't see integration details
findings.fix_direction: Add can(integration.manage) check

findings.what: Root CONTEXT.md says 12 bounded contexts — actually 13. Notification context listed but doesn't exist in code
findings.why: Spec lies about context count and includes vaporware
findings.fix_direction: Update count to 13, mark Notification as Planned or remove

findings.what: Cross-context table missing Activity row
findings.why: Spec doesn't document an entire existing context
findings.fix_direction: Add Activity to context table

findings.what: Events consumed table missing on-reply-submitted handler
findings.why: Incomplete event documentation
findings.fix_direction: Add on-reply-submitted to table

findings.what: Route tree missing people.tsx, goals.tsx, metrics.tsx routes. properties/new.tsx listed but doesn't exist
findings.why: Doc doesn't match actual route structure
findings.fix_direction: Update route tree to match actual files

findings.what: Missing inbox/ directory, use-staff-property-id hook, form-with-field, image-upload-field, multiple layout files
findings.why: Significant components undocumented
findings.fix_direction: Add missing items to folder structure

findings.what: Phase 10 describes periodic polling (every 15 min) — sync was actually implemented via Pub/Sub push
findings.why: Outdated plan doc
findings.fix_direction: Update Phase 10 to reflect Pub/Sub implementation

findings.what: Only lists 2 facade ports — code has 4 (ReviewStatsPort, MetricStatsPort, PortalMetricsPort, StaffPortalResolverPort)
findings.why: ADR incomplete
findings.fix_direction: Add missing ports to ADR 0007

findings.what: TOCTOU race in draftReply: finds existing reply (L69), checks status (L77), then upserts (L83/95). Two concurrent draftReply calls can both pass the status check before either writes, allowing overwriting a submitted/approved reply.
findings.why: Lost update if two users draft a reply for the same review simultaneously
findings.fix_direction: Use DB-level conditional UPDATE WHERE status IN ('draft','rejected') — check affected row count

findings.what: TOCTOU race in submitReply: reads reply (L130), validates transition (L138), upserts (L146). Concurrent submit+approve could both pass validation.
findings.why: Status machine can be violated under concurrent access — reply could skip states
findings.fix_direction: Atomic UPDATE WHERE id=? AND status='draft' — if 0 rows affected, throw transition error

findings.what: Same TOCTOU pattern in approveReply (L182 validate, L198 upsert), rejectReply (L240 validate, L256 upsert), retryPublish (L415 validate, L431 upsert). All reply operations read-then-write without atomicity.
findings.why: Entire reply lifecycle vulnerable to concurrent state corruption
findings.fix_direction: All reply mutations need atomic conditional UPDATE WHERE status=current_expected

findings.what: New counter decrement is done in a sequential loop — each decrement is a separate Redis eval call. For 100 items transitioning from 'new', that's 100 Redis round-trips.
findings.why: O(n) Redis calls per bulk update — slow and wasteful for large batches
findings.fix_direction: Add decrementBy(count) to NewCounterPort, single Redis call with Lua DECRBY

findings.what: getContainer() uses simple if(!_container) check — not safe under concurrent first requests in serverless/worker environments. Two simultaneous cold starts could create two containers.
findings.why: In serverless, two concurrent cold-start invocations could initialize container twice, causing duplicate event handler registrations
findings.fix_direction: Use module-level eager initialization or double-checked lock pattern

findings.what: goalRepoEarly = _createGoalRepo(db) creates a SEPARATE repo instance from what buildGoalContext creates internally. If GoalContext internally creates its own goalRepo, there are two repository instances for the same table.
findings.why: Potential cache inconsistency if repos have in-memory state. Wasteful duplicate connection
findings.fix_direction: Verify buildGoalContext accepts an external repo, or share the same instance

findings.what: setCount sets 24h TTL but increment/decrement don't refresh TTL. After initial set, the counter auto-expires in 24h even if actively used.
findings.why: Counter silently resets to 0 after 24h even if new items are constantly arriving — stale display
findings.fix_direction: Refresh TTL on increment/decrement, or don't set TTL (DB is source of truth for backfill)

findings.what: onReplyPublished bypasses canTransition/validateTransition — only checks if status is 'addressed' or 'archived' before forcing to 'addressed'. A 'new'→'addressed' transition is technically valid per rules.ts, but 'read'→'addressed' is also forced without validation.
findings.why: Inconsistent with the domain rules — event handler ignores the state machine
findings.fix_direction: Use validateTransition from domain/rules.ts before updating status

findings.what: handleDragEnd fires category reorder for ALL drag events including link drags — nested DnD contexts bubble up
findings.why: arrayMove with -1,-1 indices silently corrupts sort order and fires spurious server call
findings.fix_direction: Early return if oldIndex === -1 or newIndex === -1

findings.what: recordScanFn has no rate limiting — public unauthenticated endpoint can be flooded
findings.why: submitRating/submitFeedback have rate limiting but recordScan was missed
findings.fix_direction: Add rateLimiter.check() to recordScanFn

findings.what: activityFromRow casts DB action/resourceType with 'as' assertions without validation — corrupted rows pass through
findings.why: Invalid enum values from DB migrations surface in UI silently
findings.fix_direction: Validate against ALLOWED_ACTIONS/ALLOWED_RESOURCE_TYPES on read path

findings.what: queryAggregate double-applies periodEnd AND rollingWindowDays simultaneously — intersection likely empty
findings.why: Independent conditions pushed without mutual exclusion
findings.fix_direction: Use else-if: periodEnd OR rollingWindowDays, not both

findings.what: DnD reorder optimistic update not rolled back on server error — catch only logs
findings.why: UI shows wrong order and diverges from server after failure
findings.fix_direction: Save snapshot before optimistic update, restore on error

findings.what: Activity queries use can(role,'inbox.manage') for admin-wide visibility but activity logs span reviews, replies, notes — not just inbox
findings.why: Staff with inbox.read but not inbox.manage see filtered activity even for non-inbox resources
findings.fix_direction: Use broader admin check or dedicated activity.admin permission

findings.what: property.review allows 'sum' aggregation for rating values — summing 1-5 star ratings is meaningless, should be 'avg'
findings.why: Default sum produces useless numbers like '847 total rating'
findings.fix_direction: Change to ['count','avg','max'] with default 'avg'

findings.what: useEffect with refreshKey dependency causes double fetch on first render when refreshKey is provided
findings.why: Immediate load + 2s delayed load both fire on mount
findings.fix_direction: Separate mount effect from refresh-key effect

findings.what: Honeypot hidden input has no name/value binding — dead HTML that doesn't contribute to bot detection
findings.why: Server-side honeypot check only validates JSON payload field, not the HTML input
findings.fix_direction: Either wire to React state or remove the dead HTML

findings.what: Dashboard route has NO beforeLoad permission check — any authenticated user (including Staff) can access the dashboard route directly. No can() check for 'dashboard.read' or any permission.
findings.why: Staff role should not see the admin dashboard — relies entirely on UI hiding the link, not server-side enforcement
findings.fix_direction: Add beforeLoad with can(role, 'dashboard.read') or equivalent permission check

findings.what: Property import route has NO beforeLoad permission check — any authenticated user can access Google connection management and trigger imports
findings.why: Staff can connect Google accounts and trigger GBP imports if they know the URL
findings.fix_direction: Add beforeLoad with can(role, 'property.create') or 'integration.manage'

findings.what: Team detail route has NO beforeLoad permission check — any authenticated user can view team settings and members by navigating to /properties/{id}/teams/{id}
findings.why: Staff role can view team composition and settings without staff_assignment.read permission
findings.fix_direction: Add beforeLoad with can(role, 'team.read') or 'staff_assignment.read'

findings.what: Properties list route has NO beforeLoad permission check — shows all properties including delete button to any authenticated user
findings.why: Staff sees delete property buttons (UI hides them but route is still accessible directly)
findings.fix_direction: Add beforeLoad with can(role, 'property.read') or at minimum check authenticated

findings.what: Property layout route has NO beforeLoad permission check — any authenticated user can access any property's sub-routes if they know the propertyId
findings.why: Cross-tenant/cross-property access possible if user guesses UUIDs — no ownership validation in route guard
findings.fix_direction: Add beforeLoad checking user has access to this specific propertyId

findings.what: Guest session cookie set via document.cookie — no HttpOnly flag. sessionId visible to any JS on the page (including XSS). Also, the session ID is generated client-side with crypto.randomUUID() — a malicious client can send any sessionId.
findings.why: Session fixation/hijacking risk — attacker can set arbitrary sessionIds or read them via XSS
findings.fix_direction: Generate sessionId server-side and set cookie with HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite flags

findings.what: recordScan called in useEffect with empty deps but references 'portal' variable not in deps array. Also, deps=[...] is missing recordScan, source, and portal.id — React will use stale closures on re-renders
findings.why: If portal changes (route params change), scan still records with old portal.id
findings.fix_direction: Add proper deps or use a ref for the scan call

findings.what: getReplyPerformance joins replies→reviews on reviewId but only filters replies.organizationId and reviews.propertyId — does NOT filter reviews.organizationId. If a review was moved between organizations, wrong data could leak.
findings.why: Tenant isolation gap in dashboard aggregation query
findings.fix_direction: Add eq(reviews.organizationId, organizationId) to the reply join WHERE clause

findings.what: avgRating uses Number(rows[0]?.avgRating ?? 0) but PostgreSQL avg() returns null for empty sets, and Number(null) is 0. This is actually correct but Number(undefined) would be NaN — the ?? operator handles null but not undefined from Drizzle
findings.why: Defensive — Drizzle can return undefined for empty result sets
findings.fix_direction: Use Number(rows[0]?.avgRating ?? 0) is fine, but add explicit null check

findings.what: Idempotency check uses payload object for duplicate detection — but JavaScript object comparison. If findDuplicate serializes to JSON for comparison, key ordering could cause false negatives (same logical payload treated as different)
findings.why: Duplicate activity logs could be created if payload key order varies between calls
findings.fix_direction: Use deterministic JSON.stringify with sorted keys, or hash the payload for comparison

findings.what: Cursor deserialization silently swallows malformed input — treated as first page. An attacker could craft a cursor that decodes to arbitrary JSON (e.g., {limit: 999999}) and the parsed value is used directly in the query
findings.why: Deserialized cursor values are used without validation — could inject unexpected filter parameters
findings.fix_direction: Validate the parsed cursor against a schema before using it

findings.what: Event bus uses Promise.allSettled with inner try/catch — the allSettled result is never inspected. Failed handlers are caught by inner try/catch and logged, but the allSettled wrapper means errors are double-handled. More importantly, the emit() never knows if handlers succeeded.
findings.why: Silent handler failures — emitter never knows if critical side effects (counter decrement, activity log) failed
findings.fix_direction: Consider returning handler results or at minimum logging which specific handlers failed

findings.what: validateUrl uses new URL() which accepts any valid URL including javascript:, data:, and file: protocols. If this URL is rendered in an href, it's an XSS vector.
findings.why: Portal links could contain javascript: URLs that execute when clicked
findings.fix_direction: Add protocol check — only allow http: and https: URLs

findings.what: isValidExternalUrl only checks https: protocol — but validateUrl (used during creation) allows any protocol. Inconsistency: link creation allows http:/javascript: but smart routing check only accepts https:
findings.why: A link could be created with javascript: URL but would fail the external URL check in smart routing
findings.fix_direction: Align validateUrl to also restrict to https: (and optionally http: for dev)

findings.what: getRecentReviews uses correlated EXISTS subqueries in the reply_status CASE — runs 2 subqueries per review row. For a property with 1000 reviews, that's 2000 subqueries.
findings.why: O(n) subquery performance — slow for properties with many reviews
findings.fix_direction: Use LEFT JOIN on replies with aggregation instead of EXISTS subqueries

findings.what: softDeleteTeam checks permissions and existence but does NOT check if team has active staff assignments. Deleting a team with active assignments leaves orphaned assignments pointing to a soft-deleted team.
findings.why: Data inconsistency — staff assignments reference a deleted team. The UI would show broken team references
findings.fix_direction: Either reject delete if assignments exist, or cascade nullify teamId on assignments

findings.what: Comment says '// 5. Persist' but steps 3 and 4 are missing — numbering jumps from 2 to 5
findings.why: Confusing for maintainers — suggests deleted logic
findings.fix_direction: Fix comment numbering to be sequential

findings.what: Goal server functions check can(ctx.role, 'goal.read/create/update/cancel') but do NOT verify the goal belongs to the user's organizationId. The use case gets orgId from ctx but if the goal ID in the input belongs to a different org, the DB query may or may not filter by orgId.
findings.why: Potential cross-tenant goal access if repo queries don't enforce orgId
findings.fix_direction: Verify goal repo queries always include organizationId filter

findings.what: removeMember does NOT check if the target is the last AccountAdmin in the org. An admin can remove themselves (or the only other admin), leaving the org with no admin. No self-removal guard either.
findings.why: Organization becomes unmanageable — no one left with AccountAdmin role to invite/manage members
findings.fix_direction: Add invariant check: count admins in org, reject if target is the last admin. Also reject self-removal if that would leave 0 admins

findings.what: updateMemberRole persists the role change and then emits an event — but does NOT check if the role change would leave the org with 0 AccountAdmins. Demoting the last admin to PropertyManager or Staff makes the org unmanageable.
findings.why: Same as F142 — last-admin demotion leaves org without admin access
findings.fix_direction: Add invariant: if newTargetRole != 'AccountAdmin' and target is the last admin, reject

findings.what: Classic TOCTOU slug race: slugExists() checks uniqueness, then insert() happens later. Two concurrent createProperty calls with same name can both pass the slugExists check, then one fails at DB level. The error isn't caught as a duplicate slug.
findings.why: Concurrent property creation with same name causes unhandled DB error instead of friendly 'slug_taken' error
findings.fix_direction: Catch DB unique constraint violation on insert and map to slug_taken error, or use INSERT ON CONFLICT

findings.what: mirrorReply: when Google has a reply and a google_sync reply exists, the sync updates the reply text but preserves existing status/approvedBy/rejectedBy fields. If a draft reply was in progress locally and Google sync overwrites it, the local draft is lost but its status/submittedAt/approvedAt metadata persists on the google_sync row.
findings.why: Data inconsistency — google_sync reply has stale approval metadata from a previous local draft that was overwritten
findings.fix_direction: When overwriting with google_sync source, reset approval fields to null since the source is external

findings.what: computeProgressValue for 'max' uses -Infinity as initial value. If all rows have negative values (possible for diff-based metrics), the result is correct. But if rows is empty, returns 0 — which could be misleading for a max aggregation (max of nothing is undefined, not 0).
findings.why: Minor: empty dataset shows 0 progress for MAX aggregation, which could trigger false goal completion if target is 0
findings.fix_direction: Consider returning null/undefined for empty rows on max aggregation, or document that 0 is intentional

findings.what: ENCRYPTION_KEY is read from env as hex string. If the env var is not set, Buffer.from(undefined, 'hex') produces an empty buffer, and key.length !== 32 throws. But the error message leaks the expected key format — helpful for attackers.
findings.why: Error message reveals encryption scheme details (AES-256-GCM, 32 bytes, hex format)
findings.fix_direction: Use generic error message like 'Invalid encryption configuration' without format details

findings.what: Registration creates user first, then org. If org creation fails (step 3-4), the user account exists but has no org. The error message says 'Account created, but organization setup failed' but there's no recovery flow — user can't re-register (email taken) and can't create org (no UI for it if not authenticated).
findings.why: User is stranded with an account but no organization — cannot use the app at all
findings.fix_direction: Either (a) wrap in a transaction/compensating action that deletes the user on org failure, or (b) provide a recovery flow for users with accounts but no org

findings.what: Webhook always returns 200 even on internal errors (catch block returns 500, but the normal path returns 200 for all outcomes). Google Pub/Sub will not retry on 200, meaning a successfully enqueued but failed-to-process notification is silently dropped. But more critically: there's NO replay protection — the same messageId can trigger multiple sync jobs (the jobId is 'webhook:{messageId}' but BullMQ may still add duplicates if the previous job completed).
findings.why: Duplicate review sync jobs waste API quota and can cause race conditions on upsert
findings.fix_direction: Add dedup store (Redis SET with TTL) for processed messageIds, or use BullMQ job deduplication with attempts

findings.what: generatePropertySlug generates slug as `${normalizeSlug(businessName)}-${hashFn(gbpPlaceId).slice(0,8)}`. If the business name normalizes to empty string (e.g., name is all special chars like '!!!'), the slug becomes '-{hash}' which starts with a hyphen — likely violating slug constraints in the DB.
findings.why: Property import could fail with invalid slug format when business name contains no alphanumeric chars
findings.fix_direction: Add fallback: if normalizeSlug returns empty, use 'property' as base

findings.what: Groups expiring reviews by propertyId:connectionId:externalLocationId but if externalLocationId is null/undefined for any review, it creates key 'propId:connId:undefined' — all reviews without externalLocationId get grouped into one sync job with locationName=undefined, which will fail at the Google API call.
findings.why: Reviews with missing externalLocationId (edge case from manual DB inserts or migration) cause sync job failures
findings.fix_direction: Skip reviews where externalLocationId is null/undefined, log a warning

findings.what: Token refresh uses expiresIn from Google's response to compute tokenExpiresAt. If Google returns expiresIn=0 or a very small value, tokenExpiresAt will be in the past, causing an immediate refresh loop on the next call.
findings.why: Google API can return expiresIn=0 during token revocation — the code would loop trying to refresh a revoked token
findings.fix_direction: Validate expiresIn > minimum threshold (e.g., 60 seconds) before accepting; handle refresh failure gracefully

findings.what: Review sync processes reviews sequentially in a for loop. For a location with 500+ reviews, this means 500 sequential DB upserts + 500 event emits. No batching, no concurrency.
findings.why: Slow sync — a location with many reviews could take minutes, blocking the BullMQ worker
findings.fix_direction: Batch upserts and use Promise.all with concurrency limit for independent reviews

findings.what: Cancelling a recurring template goal first cancels all instances (cancelByParent) then updates the parent to cancelled. These are NOT in a transaction — if the parent update fails, instances are cancelled but the parent stays active. Conversely, if cancelByParent fails partway, some instances are cancelled while others remain active.
findings.why: Partial cancellation leaves recurring goal in inconsistent state — some instances cancelled, parent still active
findings.fix_direction: Wrap cancelByParent + parent update in a DB transaction, or reverse order (cancel parent first, then instances)

findings.what: Recurring instance spawn has TOCTOU race: checks for existing instance with listInstances() then inserts. Two concurrent cron runs (or a cron + manual trigger) can both pass the duplicate check and create two instances for the same period. The code itself flags this with TODO(review/G0-11) for a unique DB constraint.
findings.why: Duplicate recurring goal instances for the same period — double-counts progress
findings.fix_direction: Add unique DB constraint on (parentGoalId, periodStart) as the TODO suggests, and catch duplicate key errors

findings.what: Creating a recurring template goal persists the template AND spawns the first instance, but not atomically (flagged with TODO(review/G0-11)). If instance creation fails, the template exists with no instance for the current period.
findings.why: Recurring goal template has no instance for the first period — progress tracking starts empty
findings.fix_direction: Wrap template + first instance creation in a transaction

findings.what: F124 (dashboard route missing auth guard) is a FALSE POSITIVE. The Staff role has 'dashboard.read' permission in the permission matrix. The dashboard server functions check can(ctx.role, 'dashboard.read') which Staff passes. The route being accessible to Staff is by design.
findings.why: Correction — Staff CAN see the dashboard per the permission matrix
findings.fix_direction: Mark F124 as by_design

findings.what: Rejected reply's onEditResubmit callback is a no-op: `onEditResubmit={() => {}}`. The domain state machine allows rejected → draft transition, but the UI never triggers it. Users see a button that does nothing when trying to re-edit a rejected reply.
findings.why: Rejected replies cannot be re-drafted through the UI — dead-end for users
findings.fix_direction: Wire onEditResubmit to call the draft-reply server function with the existing reply content, triggering the rejected → draft transition

findings.what: Cookie consent banner is purely cosmetic. The guest session cookie (sessionId) is set by the server regardless of consent state. The banner dismisses by writing to localStorage, but no code checks consent before setting cookies. The banner text says 'We use a session cookie' but there's no actual consent enforcement.
findings.why: Misleading consent — cookie is set before user consents. May violate cookie consent regulations (GDPR/ePrivacy)
findings.fix_direction: Either gate the session cookie behind consent (don't set until consented), or remove the banner if the cookie is strictly necessary and exempt

findings.what: buildPortalLink constructor uses validateUrl (accepts any valid URL including javascript:, data:) instead of isValidExternalUrl (HTTPS only). While use cases (create-link, update-link) add the HTTPS check, the domain constructor itself is permissive. If anyone calls the constructor directly without the use-case check, XSS is possible.
findings.why: Defense-in-depth gap — domain layer should enforce HTTPS-only for external links
findings.fix_direction: Change constructor to use isValidExternalUrl or a stricter validateExternalUrl that requires HTTPS

findings.what: Tenant cache keyed by raw cookie header with 5s TTL. After org switch (setActiveOrg), the cookie changes but stale entries with old orgId persist for up to 5 seconds. clearTenantCache only removes expired entries, not the specific stale key.
findings.why: Up to 5s of stale org context after switching organizations — server functions may operate on wrong tenant
findings.fix_direction: On org switch, call resetTenantCache() or clear all entries (not just expired ones)

findings.what: The feedback table has no unique constraint on (sessionId, portalId). Unlike ratings which have `ratings_session_portal_unique` to prevent duplicate ratings per session per portal, feedback allows unlimited submissions per session per portal. A malicious or buggy client can flood feedback entries.
findings.why: No dedup protection for guest feedback — spam vector
findings.fix_direction: Add uniqueIndex on (sessionId, portalId) to match ratings, or add rate limiting at the use-case level if multiple feedback entries per portal are intentional

findings.what: audit_logs table has no indexes on organizationId or userId. This table grows unboundedly (every significant action). Querying audit logs by org (the primary access pattern) causes full table scans.
findings.why: Audit log queries degrade to O(n) full scans as the table grows
findings.fix_direction: Add index on (organizationId) and (userId) to match query patterns

findings.what: Materialized view refresh uses REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW without CONCURRENTLY. This locks the view exclusively during refresh, blocking all reads. For daily/weekly metrics on a growing dataset, this causes brief query failures or timeouts.
findings.why: Read queries against metric MVs will block/fail during refresh window
findings.fix_direction: Use REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY (requires unique index on the MV) to allow reads during refresh

findings.what: findAllActive() loads ALL active goals across ALL orgs into memory. At scale (thousands of orgs × dozens of goals), this could consume significant memory and cause GC pressure.
findings.why: Potential OOM risk at scale — all active goals loaded at once
findings.fix_direction: Process goals in batches (cursor-based pagination by organizationId) to bound memory usage

findings.what: updateStaffPortals filters portalId=null assignments OUT of the diff (line 57), so the 'all portals' assignment created during invitation acceptance can never be removed through the UI. Step 5 only iterates currentByPortal which excludes null-portalId entries.
findings.why: Users invited with 'all portals' scope cannot have their assignments modified — the all-portal assignment is invisible to the update flow
findings.fix_direction: Handle portalId=null assignments explicitly: if input.portalIds is non-empty and a null-portalId assignment exists, soft-delete it and create specific assignments instead

findings.what: updateStaffPortals runs multiple sequential DB mutations (inserts + soft-deletes) without a transaction. If insert succeeds but soft-delete fails (or vice versa), the user ends up with duplicate or missing assignments.
findings.why: Non-atomic assignment updates can leave inconsistent state — partial application of the diff
findings.fix_direction: Wrap the diff operation (steps 4+5) in a DB transaction to ensure atomicity

findings.what: afterAcceptInvitation silently returns if propertyIds JSON parse fails. This means a new team member gets NO property assignments with zero feedback — no error logged, no retry, no notification.
findings.why: Silent failure leaves new team members with no portal assignments and no indication something went wrong
findings.fix_direction: Log a warning when JSON.parse fails so the issue is detectable in production logs. Consider queuing a retry or sending an admin notification

findings.what: softDeleteTeam doesn't check for active staff assignments referencing this team. After soft-delete, assignments still reference the deleted team, causing orphaned references in UI queries.
findings.why: Deleting a team with active assignments leaves dangling references — UI shows team-less assignments or broken team names
findings.fix_direction: Check for active assignments before allowing delete. Either block deletion (throw error) or cascade-soft-delete the assignments

findings.what: metric_readings has no index on (organizationId, portalId). Portal-scoped metric queries (dashboard analytics, portal analytics tab) filter by portalId but must use the broader (org, property) index + filter, causing unnecessary rows scanned.
findings.why: Portal analytics queries degrade as metric_readings grows — missing composite index for the portal-scoped access pattern
findings.fix_direction: Add index on (organizationId, portalId, metricKey, occurredAt) to support portal-scoped time-range queries

findings.what: gbp_import_jobs has no index on organizationId. Querying import history by org requires a full table scan.
findings.why: Import history queries will degrade as more imports accumulate across orgs
findings.fix_direction: Add index on (organizationId, createdAt) for efficient org-scoped import history lookups

findings.what: portal_groups unique index on (orgId, propertyId, name) does NOT exclude soft-deleted rows. After soft-deleting a group named 'Reception', creating a new group with the same name fails with a unique constraint violation. The name is permanently reserved by the deleted row.
findings.why: Soft-deleted groups permanently reserve their names — users can't recreate groups with previously used names
findings.fix_direction: Convert to a partial unique index: WHERE deleted_at IS NULL. In Drizzle this requires a raw SQL index or dropping the deleted row's name to a sentinel value on soft-delete

findings.what: resend-invitation.ts re-exports AcceptInvitationInput as ResendInvitationInput (line 10) but TS cannot resolve the name at line 48. Pre-push hook fails with TS2304. This prevents deploying from a clean checkout.
findings.why: Build failure blocks deployment — pre-push typecheck catches this error
findings.fix_direction: Import the type directly instead of re-exporting, or check if the DTO module path is correct
